Why RS Chimes

Small things, done properly.

Every chime is cut, tuned, sealed and knotted by hand. The difference is something you hear the first time the wind picks up.

01

Tuned by hand

Each tube is shaved and tested until the notes sit together. No two are identical.

02

Mature bamboo

Thick-walled, fully grown culms — never the thin composite of imported chimes.

03

Triple-sealed

Three coats of UV-stable oil seal so British rain runs straight off.

04

Coconut crown

A natural coconut-shell top that weathers beautifully and amplifies the tone.

05

Made to order

Nothing sits in a warehouse. Yours is built when you place the order.

06

Ships in 3 days

Hand-built, checked, boxed and on its way within three working days.

07

Gift-ready

Arrives nested in a recycled kraft box with a hand-written care card.

08

Built to last

Re-oil once a year and it will see out many winters, not just one.

How it works

From the workshop to your garden.

Step 01

Built by hand, to order

Your chime is cut, shaped and tuned by a single maker — start to finish — once you order.

Step 02

Tuned, checked & boxed

Every note is checked by ear, then the chime is nested into a gift-ready kraft box.

Step 03

Hang it in minutes

One loop of cord over a hook or branch and you are done. Free UK delivery, every order.

Made for British weather

The last sound of a good evening.

Soft, low, rounded notes — closer to a temple block than the tinny clatter of metal. The kind of sound you stop and listen to.

The craft

Mature bamboo, sealed three times over.

Garden-centre chimes are stamped out by the thousand from thin, green bamboo that splits in a season. Ours starts with fully grown, thick-walled culms — cured, hand-tuned, and sealed to stand up to years outdoors.

100%
Natural mature bamboo
By hand
Every tube tuned by ear
3×
Coats of weather seal
For every space

Indoors or out, it earns its hook.

For the garden

By the fence, porch or pergola

Weather-sealed and ready for the open air — patios, porches, balconies and the bottom of the garden where the breeze runs.

For the home

A quiet note by an open window

Hung by a window or in a hallway it becomes a calm, sculptural thing — sound when the air moves, a handsome silhouette when it doesn't.

In the box

Everything, ready to hang.

01

Five tuned bamboo tubes

Graduated lengths, hand-cut and hand-tuned to ring in harmony.

02

Natural coconut-shell crown

The weather-hardy top that holds it all together and shapes the tone.

03

Weatherproof braided cord

UV-stable black cord, knotted by hand and ready to hang straight away.

04

Hand-written care card

Simple guidance to keep your chime sounding good for years: shelter, re-oil, enjoy.

Good to know

Questions, answered.

Anything else, email [email protected] — a real person replies.

Yes. The bamboo is cured and sealed with three coats of UV-stable oil, and the cord is weatherproof. Hang it in a reasonably sheltered spot, re-oil it once a year, and it will see out many winters rather than the single season cheap imports manage.

Two things: the bamboo and the tuning. We use fully grown, thick-walled culms instead of thin young bamboo, and every tube is shaved and tested by hand until the notes ring together. Mass-produced chimes skip both steps — which is why they sound random and split within a year.

Deep, woody and rounded — closer to a temple block or marimba than the bright jangle of metal chimes. The coconut crown adds resonance, so the notes carry softly rather than clatter.

Each chime is made to order, then dispatched within three working days with free UK delivery. You'll get tracking by email the moment it leaves the workshop.

Completely. It arrives fully assembled and knotted with a loop of cord — just hook it over a branch, bracket or beam. Nothing to build, no tools needed.

It's one of our most-gifted items. Every order arrives nested in a recycled kraft box with a hand-written care card, so it presents well with no extra wrapping. The Garden Pair is popular for keeping one and giving one.

In the wild

Out in real gardens.
